prediction: The context describes **LFUDA (LFU with Dynamic Aging)** — a cache replacement policy that extends LFU by adding a dynamic age offset to prevent "cache pollution" (stale-but-popular items blocking eviction). In the REPL context, this translates to: track a mental access-count per variable; at eviction, drop the lowest-count variable; apply aging by decrementing all counts at natural checkpoints (end of scan/sub-task) to prevent early-accessed variables from becoming permanently sticky. The practical rule is: keep variables referenced more than once, discard single-use variables. This is experiment arm A20 — the frequency-axis counterpart to A1's recency-axis (LRU) — testing whether frequency-based bookkeeping helps or hinders LLM working-memory management.
